content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 26, 1993

Vera M. I. Todorov
Assistant City Attorney
City of Salinas
200 Lincoln Avenue
Salinas, CA 93901

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-072

Dear Ms. Todorov:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Hal Thompson, a member of the Salinas Community Development Block
Grant Review Committee, under the conflict-of-interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

This advice is limited to the provisions of the Act. Other
legal provisions, such as Government Code Section 1090 or the
general doctrine of incompatible offices, may have some bearing on
this situation. It is suggested that you contact the city
attorney and county counsel for advice as to these matters.

QUESTION

Is Mr. Thompson prohibited from participating in decisions to
award grant funds to a nonprofit organization for which he is a
board member?

CONCLUSION

Mr. Thompson does not have a financial interest in the
nonprofit organization. Thus, he is not prohibited from
participating in decisions to award grant funds to the nonprofit
organization.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Advice File No. A-93-072
Page Two


FACTS

Hal Thompson is an unpaid member of the Salinas Community
Development Block Grant Review Committee ("Committee"). The
Committee is charged with making recommendations to the Salinas
City Council on the allocation of Community Development Block
Grant funds for the public service activities and programs of
various nonprofit organizations in the City of Salinas. The
Committee reviews the applications and makes recommendations on
appropriate funding levels.

Mr. Thompson is also an unpaid board member for the Monterey
County Housing Council ("Council"), a nonprofit charitable
organization. The Council's purpose is to provide financial
rental assistance to low-income households in Monterey County.
The Council may be applying to the Committee for funding this
year.

In a telephone conversation, you indicated that Mr. Thompson
has not received any income or gifts from the Council. He serves
in his capacity as a nonpaid board member.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.

An official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate
family, or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

Advice File No. A-93-072
Page Three


          (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

          (e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

          For purposes of this section, indirect
investment or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public official, by an agent on behalf of a
public official, or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, the official's agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent interest
or greater.

                            Section 87103

     Accordingly, a public official may not make, participate in
making, or attempt to use his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official, or on a member of the official's immediate family, or on
any of the official's economic interests specified in Section
87103 above.

     It does not appear that Mr. Thompson has an economic interest
as specified above.  The Monterey County Housing Council is a
nonprofit organization, not a business entity.  Thus, Mr. Thompson
does not have an interest in the Council under Subsections
87103(a) or (d).  Further, since you indicated that he has not
received income or gifts from the Council, he does not have a
financial interest under Subsections 87103(c) or (e).

     Mr. Thompson does not have a financial interest in the
Monterey County Housing Council.  He is not prohibited from
participating in decisions to award block grant money.

     If however, Mr. Thompson should ever receive income or gifts
from the Council, he should refer to Commission Regulation 18702.5

Advice File No. A-93-072
Page Three


to determine when the decision would have a material financial
effect on the Council. A copy of that regulation is enclosed.

     I trust this answers your question.  If we can be of further
assistance, please feel free to contact the Commission's Legal
Division at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Jeanette E. Turvill
                                             Political Reform Consultant
                                             Legal Division

JM/JET/jt
Enclosure
